title: Model Spec
description: Model specification (HuggingFace repo or local path). Backend-agnostic.
documentation:
## Purpose
A model spec is a compact string that uniquely identifies a machine learning model and tells the system where to find it. The format is `<scheme>:<identifier>` with an optional query string for filtering (e.g. `hf:org/model-name?include=*Q8_0*`). The scheme determines which backend or repository resolves the spec.

## When to use
Use this type wherever a cap accepts a model identifier as input. Prefer the more specific tagged variants (e.g. `media:model-spec;gguf;textable;llm`) when the backend or task is known, so the routing system can match the most capable cap without ambiguity.

## Notes
The `pattern = ".*:.*"` validation requires the scheme separator colon to be present. Strings of up to 512 characters are allowed to accommodate long query strings. Local paths should use the `local:` prefix to distinguish them from repository references.
